credentials.label: developmental psychology
credentials.detail: every tool here is shaped by how people actually grow — not how curricula assume they do

credentials.label: learning design
credentials.detail: play is the medium, not the reward — everything here is designed to work through play, not around it

credentials.label: global context
credentials.detail: these tools have been played in classrooms, living rooms, and workshops across four continents

credentials.label: stealth assessment
credentials.detail: learning shows itself when the stakes are low enough — we design for that, then watch carefully

principles.heading: development, not just engagement
principles.body: fun is easy to manufacture. growth is harder. every tool here is built to meet people exactly where they are and invite them further — through age-appropriate challenge, emotional safety, and progressive depth. the engagement you see on the surface is load-bearing.

principles.heading: play is the mechanism, not the reward
principles.body: play isn't what you earn after the real work. play is the real work. it's how humans make sense of what they don't yet understand — the engine of connection, creativity, and resilience. five years of research at the LEGO foundation and decades of facilitation practice live underneath every tool here.

principles.heading: evidence checks intuition
principles.body: good design starts with curiosity and gets checked by evidence. real-time documentation and reflection are woven into every experience — not as assessment, but as care. you see what's actually happening, not what anyone hoped would happen.

bio.text: the harbour is held by winded.vertigo — and by everyone who picks something up and plays. garrett jaeger and a collective of educators, designers, and researchers built what's here. three convictions shape everything we make: play. aliveness. justice. the harbour is where they become something you can hold.

hero.title: the harbour
hero.subtitle: come in, rest your bearings, pick something up. the harbour is protected water — a still place where play is already happening and nothing needs to be proved.

cta.heading: you're already here.
cta.body: pick something up and play. no setup, no accounts — just the thing and you. the harbour is always open.

connection.heading: how the harbour stays safe
connection.body: the harbour is held by winded.vertigo — and by everyone who picks something up and plays. garrett jaeger and a collective of educators, designers, and researchers built what's here. three convictions shape everything we make: play. aliveness. justice. the harbour is where they become something you can hold.
connection.linkLabel: there is more water beyond this harbour
